title: WildFly JSR-352 Batch Job Execution Failures
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder; Splunk Add-on for JMX (`Splunk_TA_jmx`); file monitoring for `jboss:server`, structured audit for `wildfly:audit`
spl:
index=web sourcetype="jboss:server"
| regex _raw="(?i)(BATCH.*FAILED|JobRepository.*FAILURE|javax.batch.*Exception)"
| rex field=_raw "job=(?<job_name>\S+)"
| stats count by host, job_name
| where count >= 1
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: Batch jobs often power billing and reporting; silent failures skew business numbers. Centralizing JSR-352 errors aids operations handoff.
value: Reduces financial and compliance risk from missed batch completion.
grandmaExplanation: We use this to reduce financial and compliance risk from missed batch completion.
implementation: Enable batch job logging; tag batch hosts. Create ticket automation on first failure in prod windows.
visualization: Time series for pool/queue metrics, top stats for failures, event timeline for deploys.
knownFalsePositives: Spikes or gaps can follow approved maintenance, load tests, or short collection outages. We add a second signal and a change window before escalating.
